desc: Brașov is a compact Transylvanian city framed by Carpathian foothills, with a handsome medieval core, cable-car viewpoints, and quick access to castles and mountain towns. Demand is strongest in midsummer weekends and the December ski-and-festive window, while late spring and early autumn are usually the best balance of weather, value, and lighter crowds.
hls: Colorful medieval old town around Council Square
Tâmpa Mountain viewpoint above the city
Strong base for Bran, Peleș, and mountain day trips
Excellent shoulder-season value versus Western Europe
Good mix of heritage streets and outdoor access
Winter access to nearby ski areas

todo.name: Council Square and Black Church
todo.desc: Start in Piața Sfatului, then visit the Gothic Black Church and surrounding old-town lanes.

todo.name: Tâmpa cable car + viewpoint
todo.desc: Ride or hike up Tâmpa for panoramic views over Brașov and the surrounding Carpathians.

todo.name: Rope Street and Șchei district
todo.desc: Explore one of Europe's narrowest streets and the historic Romanian quarter beyond the walls.

todo.name: Bran Castle day trip
todo.desc: Visit the region's best-known castle and pair it with nearby mountain villages.

todo.name: Peleș Castle from Sinaia
todo.desc: See one of Romania's most ornate royal palaces in the Prahova Valley.

todo.name: Poiana Brașov mountain day
todo.desc: Use nearby resort lifts and trails for hiking in summer or skiing in winter.

wifi.note: Generally reliable across central hotels and apartments, with strong 4G/5G backup in town.

prac.fltNote: No consistent nonstop Gatwick-Brașov service; most UK trips route via Bucharest or one-stop European hubs. Typical returns are around £70-£150 in low season, £150-£240 in shoulder demand, and £240+ around summer peaks and Christmas/New Year windows.
prac.visa: Romania is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally visit visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: December holidays and ski weekends raise accommodation prices quickly in the Brașov-Poiana zone.
Some mountain roads and hiking routes are weather-sensitive in late autumn and winter.
Weekend rail and bus services to popular day-trip sites can fill up in summer.

sweet: May-June and September are usually the strongest all-round months; early December is atmospheric but pricier due to festive and ski demand.
